name: Wine Fish Chunks in Broth
recipeInstructions:
1. MARINATING: Clean the fish and discard the head and fins.
- Wash the fish and cut across into 1½-inch sections.
- In a bowl, mix salt and white wine.
- Add the fish chunks; rub with the marinade, cover, and refrigerate for 6 hours.
After 2 hours, turn the fish to coat evenly with the marinade.
2. PREPARATION:
- Wash the cabbage leaves and slice them down the middle into 1-inch sections.
- Rinse the tofu cubes and cut into 1-inch pieces.
- Wash the scallions; trim and shred the greens and all.
- Peel and mince the ginger.
- Heat the salad oil until it smokes. Remove from heat.
3. COOKING:
- Rinse the fish chunks and drain them.
- In a saucepan, combine chicken stock, tofu, ginger, and salt.
- Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes.
- When ready to add the fish and cabbage, increase the heat to bring to a boil; add fish and cabbage when the liquid boils. Cover the pan.
The fish and cabbage will cook in about 3-5 minutes, with the cabbage leaves turning bright lime green.
4. SERVING:
- Ladle the fish, cabbage, and tofu into a warm shallow serving bowl.
- Add soup.
- Garnish with cooked oil, minced scallion, and pepper.
- Serve.
